leads.name: North Italia
leads.expansion: Active — Cheesecake Factory funding aggressive growth
leads.why_fit: Wedge into Fox Restaurant Concepts (20-brand parent). Maggiano's sat under Brinker; North Italia sits under Cheesecake Factory — same shape.
leads.kill_risk: Procurement may be centralized at Cheesecake Factory HQ. Pitch the parent, not the brand.
leads.notes: Originally on Austin's list. Counsel said reframe as wedge into Fox Restaurant Concepts.

leads.name: Earls Kitchen + Bar
leads.expansion: Nashville Yards opened Apr 2026. Boston Seaport West 2027. Hawaii in build phase.
leads.why_fit: Independent ownership, US expansion live, polished-casual aesthetic. Strongest single-brand fit on the list.
leads.kill_risk: Canadian HQ — US procurement may run through a designer/GC relationship. Find that designer first.
leads.notes: Boston Seaport West 2027 is the live FF&E window — Q2-Q3 2026 selection.

leads.name: bartaco
leads.parent: L Catterton (PE, bundled with Barcelona Wine Bar + Uncle Julio's)
leads.expansion: One Loudoun in build now (FF&E already locked).
leads.kill_risk: PE-owned multi-concept platform consolidates procurement. Rustic-shack aesthetic intentionally cheap.
leads.contact: Mendal direct in HubSpot (warmest path per 5/15 note)
leads.notes: Counsel said drop on aesthetic + PE-owner shape. Austin asked to keep — leaving in at tier C.

leads.name: True Food Kitchen
leads.parent: Centerbridge Partners (PE) + HumanCo + Manna Tree + Oprah
leads.expansion: Capex pivoting to new fast-casual format launch.
leads.kill_risk: PE-controlled, light/plant aesthetic doesn't match HI heavy custom hardwood. Build value-engineered.
leads.notes: Counsel said drop. Austin asked to keep — tier C.

leads.name: Lazy Dog
leads.why_fit: Rocky Mountain warm interiors = custom wood territory.
leads.kill_risk: PE 13yrs in — late-stage exit mode means FF&E squeeze. Mid-tier price point.
leads.notes: Counsel split — pessimist kill, generalist keep. Treat uncertain.

leads.name: Parker Hospitality (Hampton Social)
leads.why_fit: Originally on Austin's list. Right shape eventually.
leads.kill_risk: 5 units today = $100K total opportunity vs. sales cycle cost. Revisit in 18 months.
leads.notes: Counsel said watch list, not active. Austin asked to keep.

leads.name: 1 Hotels
leads.parent: SH Hotels & Resorts (Barry Sternlicht / Starwood Capital)
leads.expansion: 19 more by 2028 (Austin, Miami, Crete, Seattle, Melbourne, Copenhagen).
leads.why_fit: Originally on Austin's list. Hotel restaurant FF&E opportunity.
leads.kill_risk: Each property bespoke-designed by different name-brand firm. ESG/sustainability promise may conflict with Vietnam manufacturing.
leads.notes: Channel-partner play (RFP Design Group, OMNIA) — never direct.

leads.name: Cooper's Hawk Winery & Restaurants
leads.expansion: Greensboro NC (Nov 2025), Madison AL (Feb 2026), Avondale AZ late 2025, Pleasant Prairie WI summer 2026.
leads.why_fit: BULLSEYE. Polished-casual, wine-club driven, every location heavy custom millwork + tabletops + banquettes. Slightly more upscale Maggiano's.
leads.kill_risk: None obvious. Multi-state white-space attack ongoing.
leads.notes: Counsel's #1 recommendation. ~6 stores/yr at $20K profit = $120K/yr recurring.

leads.name: Postino WineCafé (Upward Projects)
leads.parent: Upward Projects (PE-backed — also owns Federal Pizza, Windsor, Joyride)
leads.expansion: Doubling in 5 years. 2026: Charlotte (Plaza Midwood), Chapel Hill NC, Austin Westlake, Vernon Hills IL.
leads.why_fit: Wine-bar concept, controlled design language across the chain. Lots of tabletops + bar tops. One conversation sells into 4 brands.
leads.kill_risk: PE recently invested — capex pace will be aggressive but procurement may centralize.
leads.notes: Cross-sell potential into Federal Pizza, Windsor, Joyride.

leads.name: Fox Restaurant Concepts (parent)
leads.expansion: Flower Child + Blanco at Novus Place Tempe (Feb 2026), Doughbird Dallas, The Henry Plano late 2026, new cocktail bar concept teased.
leads.why_fit: THE WEDGE. ~20 design-forward concepts (Flower Child, Culinary Dropout, The Henry, Doughbird, North Italia). One FF&E director = 20-brand pipeline. Pitch: 'we did Maggiano's tabletops — your sibling under Cheesecake Factory.'
leads.kill_risk: Cheesecake Factory parent procurement may want to consolidate. But Maggiano's worked inside Brinker — same shape.
leads.notes: GENERALIST'S ONE PICK. Ceiling here is $20M+, not $3M. Highest leverage on the entire list.

leads.name: CAVA
leads.expansion: 72 opens in 2025, 74-76 guided for 2026. Target 1,000 by 2032.
leads.why_fit: $1B+ revenue, Mediterranean fast-casual with real design ambition — wood, stone, warm materials.
leads.kill_risk: Fast-casual = smaller tabletop budget per store. Procurement at scale may already be locked.
leads.notes: Approach via RFP Design Group / Avendra (procurement channel). Volume play even at low per-unit margin.

leads.name: Sixty Vines
leads.expansion: Fort Lauderdale (Mar 2025), Gaithersburg MD (Oct 2025), San Antonio La Cantera early 2026. Multi-state runway.
leads.why_fit: Wine country aesthetic. Wine-on-tap requires bespoke bar systems + millwork. Polished-casual.
leads.kill_risk: Smaller scale than top picks but right design tier.

leads.name: Velvet Taco
leads.expansion: London Nov 2025. 2026: Raleigh (Feb), Phoenix PV Mall, Georgetown TX (July).
leads.why_fit: Brand DNA is 'weirdly designed taco bar' — quirky tilework, custom furniture, NOT commodity.
leads.kill_risk: New PE owner may push standardization to drive multiples.

leads.name: Auberge Collection
leads.expansion: 2026: The Knox (Dallas), The Hearst (SF), The Birdsall (Houston), Moncayo (Puerto Rico), Shell Bay (Australia). 2027: Shore Club Miami Beach.
leads.why_fit: Boutique luxury hotel group. Every property = one-off design statement + attached restaurants. Major Food Group opens Major's Grill in Auberge Cambridge House London — proves they buy from custom operators.
leads.kill_risk: 1-2 properties/yr, but each = full restaurant FF&E buy. Different designer per property.
leads.notes: Per-deal dollar size is the highest in the list.

leads.name: Soho House
leads.expansion: 2026: Tokyo (spring), Los Cabos, Flatiron NYC, NY flagship full renovation, Holloway LA, Miami Beach renovation. 2027: Soho Farmhouse NY, Desert House Palm Springs, Ranch House Sonoma.
leads.why_fit: Members club + hotel. Every house custom-designed for restaurants, club rooms, hotel rooms. Already paid the design-tax.
leads.kill_risk: Each house has its own interior designer. Sale is per-property.
leads.notes: Prestige reference customer — winning one Soho House opens 1 Hotels-tier doors.

leads.name: Eataly
leads.expansion: Aventura FL, West Palm Beach FL (CityPlace), King of Prussia PA, Short Hills NJ. JFK Terminals 4/5 coming. New 'Eataly Caffè' smaller format. 20 stores in 5 years.
leads.why_fit: Italian food hall + restaurant. Multiple seating concepts per location, each architecturally distinct. Aggressive North America push.
leads.kill_risk: Italian heritage — may prefer Italian-made FF&E for brand authenticity.
leads.notes: Big per-location FF&E spend. Aventura is on home turf.

leads.name: Lettuce Entertain You (Aba + Sushi-san)
leads.parent: Lettuce Entertain You (Rich Melman, Chicago, 100+ concepts)
leads.expansion: Aba Nashville (summer 2025), Sushi-san Nashville (fall 2025), Ema Austin (fall 2025), Mediterranean concept at The Seam Dallas (late 2026), Crying Tiger Chicago.
leads.why_fit: Aba + Sushi-san are the design-forward growth vehicles. Both expanding nationally. Restaurant powerhouse with deep design budget.
leads.kill_risk: Centralized procurement could be a headwind. 100 concepts under one roof means standardization pressure.
leads.notes: Worth the call. Aba + Sushi-san are the growth vehicles, not the legacy concepts.

leads.name: Life Time Athletic
leads.expansion: Winter Park FL (Apr 2026), Paradise Valley AZ (H1 2026), Ocotillo AZ (spring 2026). NYC area doubling.
leads.why_fit: Curveball. Every Life Time has LifeCafe + bar + lounge with real dining. Athletic country club expansion = serious millwork/tabletop spend. Public co, capex flowing.
leads.kill_risk: Not a restaurant chain — fitness procurement may be in a different lane.
leads.notes: Hidden Maggiano's-shape budget inside fitness expansion. Nobody else is calling them.

leads.name: Joe & The Juice
leads.expansion: 100 new US stores in 18 months announced.
leads.why_fit: Each store has signature Scandinavian minimalist millwork — POS counters and bar furniture. Single repeatable kit, high unit count.
leads.kill_risk: Smaller dollar per store. Existing FF&E partner likely.
leads.notes: Volume play — 100 stores × $5K = $500K with one customer.
